That means wherever the quail is, the blue jay has to be somewhere to its right. Next, the cardinal is the third from the left. So position 3 is definitely the cardinal. Then, the quail is the second from the left. Oh, that's a specific position. So position 2 is the quail. Wait, if the quail is second, then the blue jay has to be to the right of that. So blue jay can be in positions 3, 4, or 5. But wait, position 3 is already the cardinal. So blue jay can't be third. Therefore, blue jay must be in position 4 or 5. Then there's another clue: the blue jay is to the left of the hummingbird. So wherever the blue jay is, the hummingbird has to be to its right. Let me try to map this out.
